title: Starbucks tests TikTok Creator Network for employee content
tldr: Starbucks is paying its baristas to create TikTok content through a formal Creator Network, formalizing employee-generated content as a paid brand channel. This signals a shift from ad-hoc EGC to structured, incentivized programs other brands will copy.
angle: How paying employees to create content changes governance, authenticity and the economics of EGC

title: Study: How embedded followers can boost influencer campaigns
tldr: New research shows influencer posts earn up to 5x more retweets when seeded through a few well-networked followers rather than broad reach. Reframes influence as network topology, not just follower count.
angle: Why network position beats follower count — rethinking how content actually spreads

title: Forecasting the future: how can publishers thrive in the AI search era?
tldr: With 42% of UK searches now generating AI summaries and zero-click behaviour rising, the traffic model that funded a decade of publishing is being dismantled. A clear-eyed look at how publishers must adapt content strategy.
angle: What content strategists should build when clicks disappear and AI answers dominate discovery

title: D&AD New Blood Awards 2026: a student project about periods wins both top prizes
tldr: Blood Stocks, a Danish proposal to turn menstruation into a medical asset, swept both the Black and White Pencil — a human, taboo-breaking idea beating polished commercial work. A signal of where creative craft and purpose are heading.
angle: Why raw human ideas about taboo topics are outperforming polished craft in creative awards

title: Publishers take on gen-AI giants… with CCJs
tldr: Small publishers are using an army of small-claims court cases to force AI companies to pay for scraped content. A novel grassroots legal tactic against Google and others flattening the publishing economy.
angle: The small-claims rebellion: a new front in the content-vs-AI value war

title: Bobcat's CMO says AI search is rewriting the customer journey
tldr: Bobcat's CMO details how LLM-driven search is reshaping content strategy and the B2B customer journey, forcing brands to optimize for AI citation over traditional SEO. A concrete enterprise view of the AEO shift.
angle: What a real CMO is changing in content ops to survive AI search

title: Beyond news deserts: How Village Media is proving local journalism can still grow
tldr: Village Media offers a working counter-narrative to local-news collapse, showing a scalable, profitable model for community journalism. A rare optimistic media business-model case study.
why_fresh: working growth model in a declining sector
angle: What corporate content teams can learn from a local-news model that actually grows

title: The PESO Model Diagnostic: What Peppa Pig Can Teach You About Campaign vs System
tldr: Gini Dietrich dissects the Peppa Pig pregnancy campaign to show the difference between a one-off campaign and an integrated media system across paid, earned, shared and owned. A practical integration breakdown with a memorable case.
why_fresh: applies established framework to fresh case
angle: Campaign vs system: using a viral case to teach integrated communications thinking

title: Fifth Avenue food hall identity highlights how a person can trump a mood board
tldr: Love & War's identity for Shaver Hall shows how naming, history and a rediscovered forgotten icon can turn adaptive reuse into a human-centred brand story. A tidy branding case study prioritizing people over polish.
why_fresh: solid but routine branding case study
angle: How a single human character can anchor a brand narrative better than a mood board

title: SKIMS Menswear Campaign With Will Ferrell Blends Fictional Sports and Brand Storytelling
tldr: SKIMS builds a menswear launch around Will Ferrell and invented sports, blending comedy and fictional world-building into brand storytelling. A campaign breakdown of narrative-driven celebrity marketing.
angle: Fictional world-building as a brand storytelling device

title: Why brands are bringing creators to the World Cup sidelines
tldr: Brands are embedding creators at World Cup events to boost engagement, reach new audiences and join the cultural conversation in real time. A snapshot of creator-led event marketing at scale.
angle: Creators as the new event press corps — implications for earned vs owned coverage

title: The UAE wants kids off social media. Will Big Tech rethink its business model?
tldr: UAE moves to keep kids off social platforms, part of a global wave (56% of US adults back under-16 bans) pressuring platform business models. A signal of shifting regulation around youth media consumption.
angle: How youth-access bans could reshape where brands reach the next generation

title: Как руководителю не выгореть на старте: советы от Flowwow
tldr: Seven practical tips for new managers on setting boundaries, dropping people-pleasing and avoiding burnout, drawn from Flowwow's leadership experience. Useful for internal comms and leadership development angles.
angle: Boundary-setting for first-time leaders as an internal-comms and change-management topic

title: Leaders weigh in on how AI is changing creativity at their businesses
tldr: CEOs and marketing chiefs from Nespresso, Luma AI, PMI and Publicis Sapient discuss how AI is reshaping creative work across sectors. Executive perspectives on integrating AI into creative and content operations.
angle: Where executives actually draw the human-vs-AI line in creative work
